Here is your six-month wedding planning schedule designed to keep you on track toward your special day.

**Month 1: Foundation & Location**
Begin by establishing a budget and defining your preferred style for the ceremony and reception. Secure your chosen **location**, as this dictates the date availability and catering options. Once the site is confirmed, start narrowing down potential vendors like caterers and photographers.

**Month 2: Invitations & RSVPs**
With your **location** locked in, finalize the guest count. Compile the initial **guest list** and send save-the-date cards to those most important to you. Begin reaching out to your top-tier caterer for menu tastings at the **location**.

**Month 3: Vendors & Details**
Book major vendors, including florists, musicians, and transportation services. Confirm all details regarding accommodations for out-of-town family members. Send formal invitations to your immediate circles.

**Month 4: Attire & Finalizing Menus**
Schedule fittings for your wedding dress and suits. Work with your planner to refine the food selections. Review dietary restrictions against the final headcount on your spreadsheet.

**Month 5: Logistics & Rehearsal**
Confirm travel and lodging arrangements for guests. Organize transportation logistics for the big day. Walk through the floor plans to ensure every detail aligns perfectly with your vision.

**Month 6: Final Preparations**
Pack personal items and gifts for the bridal party. Conduct a final run-through of the schedule. Relax and prepare for the celebration!<|im_end|>

final_answer:
Here is your six-month wedding planning schedule designed to keep you on track toward your special day.

**Month 1: Foundation & Location**
Begin by establishing a budget and defining your preferred style for the ceremony and reception. Secure your chosen **location**, as this dictates the date availability and catering options. Once the site is confirmed, start narrowing down potential vendors like caterers and photographers.

**Month 2: Invitations & RSVPs**
With your **location** locked in, finalize the guest count. Compile the initial **guest list** and send save-the-date cards to those most important to you. Begin reaching out to your top-tier caterer for menu tastings at the **location**.

**Month 3: Vendors & Details**
Book major vendors, including florists, musicians, and transportation services. Confirm all details regarding accommodations for out-of-town family members. Send formal invitations to your immediate circles.

**Month 4: Attire & Finalizing Menus**
Schedule fittings for your wedding dress and suits. Work with your planner to refine the food selections. Review dietary restrictions against the final headcount on your spreadsheet.

**Month 5: Logistics & Rehearsal**
Confirm travel and lodging arrangements for guests. Organize transportation logistics for the big day. Walk through the floor plans to ensure every detail aligns perfectly with your vision.

**Month 6: Final Preparations**
Pack personal items and gifts for the bridal party. Conduct a final run-through of the schedule. Relax and prepare for the celebration!<|im_end|>
